Title: Sterols are required for the coordinated assembly of lipid droplets in developing seeds

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Oleosin family lipid droplet structural protein
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaf lipid droplets; developing embryos; seeds
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes formation of small oleosin-coated lipid droplets and supports TAG accumulation/stability; reduced oleosin abundance is associated with enlarged LDs and lower seed oil
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OLE1-GFP overexpression enabled visualization of LD phenotypes and normally produced abundant small clustered LDs in leaves. 2. In sterol-deficient dwf5 and dwf7 backgrounds, OLE1-coated LDs were drastically reduced despite TAG being inducible by oleic acid feeding, indicating failure of coordinated oleosin deposition onto LDs. 3. OLE1 transcript level strongly correlated with LD abundance across multiple LD-deficient mutants. 4. OLE1-GFP protein abundance in developing embryos was markedly decreased in dwf5 and dwf7 backgrounds.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Seed-specific/endogenous oleosin genes are expressed in developing seeds and floral organs; not normally in leaves
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: High in developing embryos/seeds; transgene constitutively expressed in leaves in OLE1-GFP line
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Lipid droplet surface; inserted via endoplasmic reticulum
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic overexpression of OLE1-GFP; fluorescence/confocal microscopy; qRT-PCR; immunoblotting; LD protein SDS-PAGE; oleic acid feeding
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Energy storage in seeds and vegetative tissues
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Ectopic overexpression in leaves forms clustered LDs and may not reflect native leaf oleosin biology
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful LD structural engineering component, but effective oil enhancement requires coordinated sterol-supported LD assembly rather than oleosin expression alone
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Whole-genome sequencing for positional cloning, SRA: PRJNA760508
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper indicates OLE1 is not the primary causative gene of the sterol phenotype; oleosin transcript/protein reduction is interpreted largely as secondary to impaired LD assembly.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Reduces sterol ester content but does not significantly affect LD abundance or TAG content in leaves and seeds
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. psat1 mutants showed major reductions in sterol ester levels. 2. Despite reduced sterol esters, psat1/OLE1 mutants displayed no significant changes in leaf or seed LD abundance or TAG content. 3. Therefore, lowered SEs are not responsible for the LD-deficient phenotype in sterol mutants.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: T-DNA mutants; sterol ester measurements by GC-MS; LD imaging; TAG quantification
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Sterol ester storage form in LDs
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Indicates manipulating sterol esterification alone is unlikely to improve seed oil packaging phenotypes
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Whole-genome sequencing for positional cloning, SRA: PRJNA760508
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a tested core metabolism-related gene because the study experimentally ruled out SE deficiency as causal.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Shared downstream Δ5-sterol-forming branch leading to campesterol/sitosterol/stigmasterol
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Δ5-sterols including campesterol, sitosterol, stigmasterol; contributes to isofucosterol-related pool
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Introduces the C5-C6 double bond into Δ7 sterol intermediates to form Δ5 sterols
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies essential membrane sterol species required for proper TAG packaging and oleosin-coated LD formation
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step for generation of Δ5-sterols required for LD biogenesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaf and seed lipid droplets; developing embryos; mature seeds
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports membrane properties and microdomain organization required for LD assembly and stable oil storage
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of DWF5 causes severe depletion of major Δ5 sterols, reduced LD number, enlarged LD size, decreased leaf TAG, accelerated TAG turnover in OLE1 background, reduced seed oil content, decreased oleosin abundance, and wrinkled seeds
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. M1-7 was mapped to DWF5 and renamed dwf5-10/OLE1; an independent T-DNA allele dwf5-8 reproduced the LD-deficient phenotype. 2. Complementation with 35S:DWF5 restored growth and LD abundance. 3. dwf5 mutants showed strong depletion of sitosterol, campesterol, stigmasterol, and isofucosterol, identifying Δ5-sterol deficiency as causal. 4. DWF5 disruption strongly reduced leaf TAG/LD accumulation in the OLE1 line but did not markedly alter intrinsic TAG synthesis rates in the absence of OLE1. 5. In seeds, dwf5 caused lower oil content, fewer but larger LDs, decreased oleosin proteins, and wrinkled seed morphology.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Sterol-pathway genes show coordinated expression with fatty acid and TAG synthesis during embryo development
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Forward genetic screen; map-based cloning; whole-genome sequencing; T-DNA alleles; complementation with 35S:DWF5; GC-MS sterol profiling; TAG quantification; pulse-chase labeling; microscopy; TEM; immunoblotting
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Oleic acid feeding; brassinosteroid feeding or inhibition tested and found non-causal
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Dwarfism; smaller rounder wrinkled seeds; reduced seed weight; altered unusual sterol accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Maintain or restore Δ5-sterol biosynthesis to improve LD assembly and seed oil storage stability; avoid disruption of DWF5 in oilseed engineering
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Whole-genome sequencing for positional cloning, SRA: PRJNA760508
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV promoter for OLE1-GFP line; CaMV35S for DWF5 complementation
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Among tested mutants, dwf5 had the lowest free sterol level and most severe LD phenotype, supporting importance of both Δ5-sterol identity and overall sterol abundance.

Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Sterol intermediates upstream of major Δ5 sterols
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Major Δ5 sterols; loss causes accumulation of Δ7-sitosterol
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Functions in sterol desaturation required for production of major Δ5 sterols
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies essential Δ5 sterols supporting proper LD biogenesis and oil storage
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step for LD-relevant sterol composition
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaf and seed lipid droplets; mature seeds
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Membrane organization for stable LD formation and oil storage
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of DWF7 reduces major sterols, decreases leaf TAG and LDs in the OLE1 line, enlarges seed LDs, lowers seed oil and oleosin abundance, and causes smaller wrinkled seeds
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. dwf7-4 mutants had limited major sterols and accumulated unusual Δ7-sitosterol. 2. Introducing dwf7 into the OLE1 line caused drastic decreases in TAG accumulation and LD abundance in leaves. 3. BR feeding did not rescue the phenotype, supporting a BR-independent sterol role. 4. dwf7 seeds had enlarged LDs, reduced LD number, reduced oil content, and reduced oleosin abundance. 5. The phenotype was generally milder than dwf5, matching less severe depletion of 24-ethyl-Δ5-sterols.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Sterol-pathway genes are coordinately expressed with oil synthesis genes during embryo development
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: T-DNA mutant analysis; sterol GC-MS; LD microscopy; TAG quantification; oleic acid pulse-chase; TEM; LD isolation; immunoblotting
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Oleic acid feeding; 24-epibrassinolide feeding; brassinazole treatment
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Dwarfism; reduced seed weight; wrinkled seeds; altered sterol profile
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Preserve DWF7 activity to sustain Δ5-sterol composition and oil-body integrity in seeds
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Whole-genome sequencing for positional cloning context, SRA: PRJNA760508
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper text inconsistently describes DWF5 and DWF7 enzyme assignments, but experimentally both are treated as sterol-pathway genes whose disruption lowers Δ5-sterols and impairs LD biogenesis.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Second branch point controlling 24-ethyl-sterol versus 24-methyl-sterol flux
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Sterol precursors at the second branch point
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: 24-ethyl-sterol pathway products including sitosterol/stigmasterol; reduction in mutants shifts toward campesterol
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Methylates sterol intermediates to direct precursor flow toward 24-ethyl-sterol synthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Diverts flux toward 24-ethyl-Δ5-sterols required for LD biogenesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Generates sterol composition favorable for membrane environments supporting LD formation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of SMT2/3 lowers 24-ethyl-Δ5-sterols, increases campesterol, and drastically decreases leaf TAG accumulation in the OLE1 line
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. cvp1-3 smt3-1 mutants showed a fivefold increase in campesterol but a 63% decrease in 24-ethyl-Δ5-sterols with unchanged total sterol levels. 2. Introducing SMT2/3 deficiency into OLE1 caused drastic decreases in leaf TAG accumulation. 3. Results support that proper 24-ethyl-Δ5-sterol composition, not just total sterol amount, is critical for LD biogenesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Double mutant analysis; sterol profiling; TAG quantification; LD imaging
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Developmental defects associated with altered sterol balance
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Fine-tuning sterol branch flux may be necessary when engineering oil accumulation to maintain favorable campesterol/sitosterol balance
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Whole-genome sequencing for positional cloning context, SRA: PRJNA760508
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper highlights altered ratio of campesterol to sitosterol as a likely cause of LD defects in cvp1-3 smt3-1.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Shared downstream branch to campesterol and sitosterol
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts 24-methylenecholesterol to campesterol and isofucosterol to sitosterol
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Produces major sterols but mutant retains total Δ5-sterol functionality sufficient for normal LD biogenesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: DWF1 disruption alters sterol composition but does not significantly affect LD abundance, leaf TAG accumulation in OLE1, seed LD morphology, or seed TAG content
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. dwf1-2 lacks sitosterol and campesterol but accumulates immediate precursors without major change in total free sterol content. 2. Introducing dwf1-2 into OLE1 caused no significant change in leaf TAG content. 3. Seed LD size distribution and seed TAG content remained similar to wild type. 4. These data indicate isofucosterol and 24-methylenecholesterol can functionally substitute for sitosterol and campesterol in LD biogenesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: T-DNA mutant analysis; sterol GC-MS; TAG assays; LD microscopy; TEM
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Oleic acid feeding in pulse-chase assays
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Dwarf morphology due to sterol/BR defects despite little effect on LD biogenesis
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Suggests some sterol end products are interchangeable for LD formation if Δ5-sterol structural features are preserved
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Whole-genome sequencing for positional cloning context, SRA: PRJNA760508
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Important comparator demonstrating that not all sterol-pathway disruptions impair oil-body assembly.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Brassinosteroid biosynthesis downstream of sterol pathway
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes an essential step in brassinosteroid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Tested as alternative explanation rather than direct determinant of TAG accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: DWF4 disruption did not significantly alter leaf LD abundance or TAG content in the OLE1 line, arguing the sterol-LD phenotype is BR independent
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. dwf4 mutants served as BR-deficient controls. 2. Introducing dwf4 into OLE1 caused no obvious changes in leaf LD abundance or TAG content. 3. Together with BR feeding/inhibition experiments, this ruled out BR deficiency as the cause of the LD phenotype.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: T-DNA mutant analysis; LD imaging; TAG quantification; BR chemical treatment
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Demonstrates that manipulating BR synthesis is not sufficient to control LD assembly phenotype described here
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Whole-genome sequencing for positional cloning context, SRA: PRJNA760508
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because it was experimentally tested to dissect sterol versus BR causality, but it is not a positive determinant of the target nutrient phenotype.
